name: Deprecated CAPL Functions
title: Deprecated CAPL Functions
description: Searches for a variable entry in a file section.
Opens a file, finds a special section and writes the variable entry with a special value.
Registers a CAPL callback function that is called before sending a packet.
To use the CAPL functions the ETHERNET_Il.DLL must be included.
Generates a FlexRay frame and sends it to the hardware.
Converts specified bit time to an absolute time.

Calculates the authenticator (CMAC) for the given payload and freshness. This function can be used for testing.

Verifies the specified authentication information (CMAC and freshness) of the PDU. The payload is also an input parameter to the verification logic.
This callback handler is called before a node transmits a secured PDU, after all data updates and the automatic Authenticator (CMAC) calculation have been done.
This callback handler is called, when a node receives a secured PDU. Apart from the verification result, all values which have been used for verification can be analyzed.
This callback handler is called, when a node receives or transmits a secured PDU. It provides the possibility to influence the freshness value.
Checks the signal value against a condition.
return: Some functions were deprecated for a long time and were therefore
tables: Note Deprecated Function All deprecated functions are marked with . If a function is deprecated you get the CAPL Compiler notification "Function is deprecated. See help for more information". In CANoe at measurement start different scenarios are possible: The function has full functionality. The functionality of the function is limited, please see notification in the Write Window. The function no longer has any effect, please see notification in the Write Window. Removed Function Some functions were deprecated for a long time and were therefore removed (obsolete). These functions are neither known to the CAPL compiler nor to CANoe. You get the CAPL Compiler notification "unknown function" and in CANoe at measurement start a notification in the Write Window. For the display of the notifications see settings in the Options dialog.
